title: A gambling boom is reshaping New Hampshire’s casino landscape
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: New Hampshire's gambling industry expands with relaxed rules, new investments, and larger casino-style gaming facilities.
The Nash Casino opened in March 2025, becoming NH's largest 'charitable gaming' facility with 130,000 square feet.
New legislation allows cities to opt-out of hosting casinos, although existing projects are protected by a grandfather clause.

title: Boston Globe sports columnist Chris Gasper announces he’s stepping away for cancer treatment
author: John Waller
bullets: Boston Globe sports columnist Chris Gasper announced he is stepping away to begin cancer treatment.
Gasper was diagnosed with prostate cancer before last NFL season and has opted for surgery after recent scans.
Gasper expressed optimism about his prognosis and plans to return to work once treatment is complete.

title: They Might Be Giants talk their Mass. beginnings, Dunkin’ campaigns, and embracing creative challenges
author: Perry Eaton
bullets: They Might Be Giants to perform at Boston's House of Blues on June 5 and 6.
John Flansburgh and John Linnell met at Lincoln-Sudbury High School in Massachusetts.
The band's latest album, "The World Is to Dig," reflects their experimental musical style.

title: Harvard grad student workers end 40-day strike without a contract
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Harvard graduate student workers ended a 40-day strike without a new contract.
The students' union agreed to resume negotiations with Harvard on June 9 and June 23.
Harvard has increased some benefits and offered a 2.75% raise, but key demands remain unmet.

title: A.J. Brown trade grades: Did experts think Patriots gave up too much for star WR?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: New England Patriots acquired A.J. Brown from Eagles for a 2028 first-round pick and a 2027 fifth-round pick.
Trade evaluations for the Patriots vary, with grades from B to A-, praising the immediate offensive upgrade.
Philadelphia Eagles received mixed reviews, with grades from B+ to C-, focusing on the impact of losing Brown.

title: 7News anchor leaving the station after 6 years
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Amanda Crawford is leaving 7News on WHDH-TV after nearly six years with the station.
Crawford started at 7News in 2020 and has a 17-year journalism career with outlets in multiple states.
She plans to pursue a new career offering more flexibility, without details on her next move.

title: 3 ways A.J. Brown will likely impact Patriots offense, Drake Maye in 2026
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots traded a 2028 first-round and 2027 fifth-round pick to the Eagles for wide receiver A.J. Brown.
A.J. Brown's ability against man coverage is expected to enhance the Patriots' offense and benefit QB Drake Maye.
Adding Brown may cause changes in the Patriots' receiver lineup, potentially impacting players like Kayshon Boutte.

title: Norton bakery apologizes for customer-requested Hitler cake posted on Facebook
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sweet Stuff Bake Shop in Norton apologized for a cake depicting 'South Park' character Cartman as Hitler, shared on Facebook.
The bakery faced backlash online after the post and removed the photo, saying they hadn't considered global sensitivities.
Reactions to the apology varied, with some defending the bakery owner and others criticizing the cake's creation and posting.

title: How windshields can affect tinted eyeglasses
author: John Paul
bullets: Photochromic lenses don't darken in cars because modern vehicle windshields filter UV light to protect structural integrity.
A 2009 Jeep Grand Cherokee's hammering noise might be due to a worn rocker arm, lash adjuster, or camshaft damage.
Toyota Highlander excels in reliability, Kia Telluride offers upscale interiors, and Ford Explorer provides strong performance.

title: Mass. police department cancels appearance of Trump-backed ‘Freedom Truck’ over ties to right-wing organization
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Westfield Police Department canceled the 'Freedom Truck' visit due to concerns over its political affiliations.
The 'Freedom Truck' is part of the Trump-backed Freedom 250 initiative and supported by PragerU.
The event faced broader scrutiny with cancellations from artists at Freedom 250 events, including Martina McBride and Bret Michaels.
